title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Start listing stops Route 1 can pick ones care . ’ll see stops listed order Harvard Nubian. station column holds short GTFS place code; stop_name ’ll pass $stop(). Since bus directions always "outbound" (away downtown) "inbound" (toward downtown), don’t need discovery step directions.

what: Step 2: Pull headways for a single day
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Look headways MIT @ Mass Ave — busy mid-route stop — directions typical winter weekday. result already data.frame. Bind together add direction label:
code: date <- "2024-01-16"  # Tuesday  inbound  <- tm_bus_query("1")$stop("MIT @ Mass Ave")$direction("inbound")$headways(date) outbound <- tm_bus_query("1")$stop("MIT @ Mass Ave")$direction("outbound")$headways(date) inbound outbound headways_df <- bind_rows(   mutate(inbound,  direction = "Inbound (-> Nubian)"),   mutate(outbound, direction = "Outbound (-> Harvard)") )  headways_df

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: departure timestamp (current_dep_dt) comes back ISO 8601 string (e.g. "2024-01-16T09:30:00"). Convert keep daytime service window.
code: headways_df <- headways_df |>   mutate(     dep_time      = as.POSIXct(current_dep_dt, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S",                                 tz = "America/New_York"),     headway_min   = headway_time_sec / 60,     benchmark_min = benchmark_headway_time_sec / 60,     hour          = as.integer(format(dep_time, "%H"))   ) |>   filter(hour >= 6, hour <= 22) headways_df

what: Step 4: Plot headways over the day
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: look : Peaks headway mean longer waits buses – often peak (inbound demand) PM peak (outbound demand). headway 15 minutes route ’s supposed run every 8 minutes suggests gap service.
code: ggplot(headways_df, aes(x = dep_time, y = headway_min, color = direction)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "Inbound (-> Nubian)"    = "#003DA5",     "Outbound (-> Harvard)"  = "#80A8DD"   )) +   labs(     title    = "Route 1 Headways at MIT @ Mass Ave",     subtitle = paste("January 16, 2024 *", date),     x        = NULL,     y        = "Minutes between buses",     color    = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "top")

what: Step 5: Compare actual vs. scheduled headway
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: benchmark_headway_time_sec field schedule promised. Comparing actual headway shows route running behind schedule. Red bars moments actual gap buses exceeded scheduled gap. Clusters red bars often indicate bus bunching – common problem long surface routes small delays compound.
code: headways_df |>   filter(direction == "Inbound (-> Nubian)") |>   mutate(     excess_min = headway_min - benchmark_min   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = dep_time, y = excess_min)) +   geom_col(aes(fill = excess_min > 0), width = 60) +     scale_fill_manual(       values = c("FALSE" = "#80A8DD", "TRUE" = "#DA291C"),       labels = c("Under schedule", "Over schedule"),       name   = NULL     ) +     geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +     labs(       title    = "Route 1 Inbound -- Excess Wait vs. Schedule",       subtitle = "MIT @ Mass Ave * January 16, 2024",       x        = NULL,       y        = "Extra minutes beyond scheduled headway"     ) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: broader view, use $aggregate_headways(), returns daily averages rather individual events.
code: agg_df <- tm_bus_query("1")$stop("MIT @ Mass Ave")$direction("inbound")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$aggregate_headways() ggplot(agg_df, aes(x = as.Date(service_date), y = mean / 60)) +   geom_line(color = "#003DA5") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed",               color = "grey50") +   labs(     title    = "Route 1 Average Inbound Headway -- January 2024",     subtitle = "MIT @ Mass Ave",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Average headway (minutes)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Now can look stops name, try exploring routes stops: See Finding Stops Name vignette full lookup API across transit modes, including commuter rail ferry.
code: # Which bus routes are available? tm_bus_routes()  # Stops on Route 66 (Harvard to Nubian) tm_bus_query("66")$stations()  # Headways at Harvard on Route 1 -- the Northern terminus tm_bus_query("1")$stop("Harvard")$direction("inbound")$headways("2024-01-16")

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: five rapid transit lines Red, Orange, Blue, Green, Mattapan.

what: Discover: what stations are on this line?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: tm_rt_query("Red")$stations() #>                stop_name     station order branches #> 1                Alewife place-alfcl     1     A, B #> 2                  Davis place-davis     2     A, B #> 3                 Porter place-portr     3     A, B #> 4                Harvard place-harsq     4     A, B #> 5                Central place-cntsq     5     A, B #> 6            Kendall/MIT place-knncl     6     A, B #> 7            Charles/MGH place-chmnl     7     A, B #> 8            Park Street place-pktrm     8     A, B #> 9      Downtown Crossing place-dwnxg     9     A, B #> 10         South Station place-sstat    10     A, B #> 11              Broadway place-brdwy    11     A, B #> 12                Andrew place-andrw    12     A, B #> 13   JFK/UMass (Ashmont)   place-jfk   101        A #> 14            Savin Hill place-shmnl   102        A #> 15         Fields Corner place-fldcr   103        A #> 16               Shawmut place-smmnl   104        A #> 17               Ashmont place-asmnl   105        A #> 18 JFK/UMass (Braintree)   place-jfk   201        B #> 19          North Quincy place-nqncy   202        B #> 20             Wollaston place-wlsta   203        B #> 21         Quincy Center place-qnctr   204        B #> 22          Quincy Adams place-qamnl   205        B #> 23             Braintree place-brntn   206        B

what: Discover: which directions does this line use?
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Rapid transit lines use geographic direction names rather inbound/outbound.
code: tm_rt_query("Red")$directions()   # northbound / southbound #>            0            1  #> "northbound" "southbound" tm_rt_query("Green")$directions() # eastbound  / westbound #>           0           1  #> "eastbound" "westbound"

what: Build a query and fetch data
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: can chain builder calls terminal call. query object accumulates context validates input set — typos station names produce error immediately, request time.
code: # Headways at Davis, all trains (direction resolved from stop ID) tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Davis")$headways("2024-01-15")  # For travel times, specify direction (determines which platform stop ID to use) tm_rt_query("Red")$from_stop("Park Street")$to_stop("Davis")$direction("northbound")$travel_times("2024-01-15")  # Aggregate headways for January 2024 (direction filters to one platform) tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Davis")$direction("southbound")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$aggregate_headways()

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: 121 bus routes. Directions always "outbound" "inbound".

what: Discover: what stops are on this route?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: tm_bus_query("1")$stations() #>                    stop_name station order #> 1                    Harvard   hhgat     1 #> 2      Mass Ave & Putnam Ave   maput     2 #> 3 Central Square (Cambridge)   cntsq     3 #> 4             MIT @ Mass Ave     mit     4 #> 5              Hynes Station   hynes     5 #> 6     Mass Ave (Orange Line)   masta     6 #> 7      Mass Ave @ Washington   wasma     7 #> 8   Melnea Cass @ Washington   melwa     8 #> 9             Nubian Station    nubn     9

what: Build a query and fetch data
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Harvard on Route 1, inbound, on a specific day tm_bus_query("1")$stop("Harvard")$headways("2024-01-15")  # Aggregate headways at Harvard over a month tm_bus_query("1")$stop("Harvard")$direction("inbound")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$aggregate_headways()

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Commuter Rail routes identified GTFS route ID. 13 lines available.

what: Discover: what stops are on this line?
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: terminus column flags endpoints line. Terminus stations stop IDs one direction — calling $stop() terminus station issue warning alert data may incomplete one direction.
code: tm_cr_query("CR-Fairmount")$stations() #>             stop_name       station order terminus #> 1           Readville place-DB-0095     1     TRUE #> 2           Fairmount place-DB-2205     2    FALSE #> 3    Blue Hill Avenue place-DB-2222     3    FALSE #> 4       Morton Street place-DB-2230     4    FALSE #> 5       Talbot Avenue place-DB-2240     5    FALSE #> 6 Four Corners/Geneva place-DB-2249     6    FALSE #> 7       Uphams Corner place-DB-2258     7    FALSE #> 8           Newmarket place-DB-2265     8    FALSE #> 9       South Station   place-sstat     9     TRUE

what: Build a query and fetch data
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Terminus warning: call $stop("South Station") CR query, see warning — South Station terminus stop IDs one direction. query still works; warning informational.
code: # Headways at Fairmount, inbound trains (toward South Station) tm_cr_query("CR-Fairmount")$stop("Fairmount")$headways("2026-06-18")  # Travel time, Fairmount → Blue Hill Avenue, inbound tm_cr_query("CR-Fairmount")$from_stop("Fairmount")$to_stop("Blue Hill Avenue")$direction("inbound")$travel_times("2026-06-18")

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Six ferry routes available. Like bus CR, directions "outbound" "inbound".

what: Discover: what stops are on this route?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: tm_ferry_query("Boat-F1")$stations() #>                      stop_name      station order terminus #> 1           Long Wharf (North)    Boat-Long     6     TRUE #> 2                  Rowes Wharf   Boat-Rowes     5    FALSE #> 3 Logan Airport Ferry Terminal   Boat-Logan     4    FALSE #> 4               Georges Island  Boat-George     3    FALSE #> 5                         Hull    Boat-Hull     2    FALSE #> 6                      Hingham Boat-Hingham     1     TRUE

what: Build a query and fetch data
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Hingham, inbound (toward Long Wharf) tm_ferry_query("Boat-F1")$stop("Hingham")$headways("2024-06-01")

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: station name arguments case-insensitive. Line route arguments accept multiple forms ("red", "Red", "line-Red" equivalent).

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: transitmattr lets pull live historical data TransitMatters Data Dashboard API directly R. can get things like: routes stops available often trains/buses coming (headways) long vehicles sit stations (dwell times) long takes travel stops (travel times) many people riding (ridership) slow zones affecting service

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
code: # install.packages("pak")  # run this once if you don't have pak pak::pak("transitmatters/transitmattr")

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
code: library(transitmattr) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)

what: Step 1: Check that the API is working
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: anything else, ’s good practice confirm API . ’ll get back something like: see "pass" ’re good go.

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: core transitmattr query builder: create query object transit mode, chain methods set context (line, stop, date), call terminal method fetch data. four query constructors — one per transit mode: query methods return data frame ready analysis — conversion needed. typical call looks like: can also set context step step reuse query object:
code: # Headways at Harvard Square on the Red Line, January 15 2024 df <- tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Harvard")$headways("2024-01-15")  head(df) q <- tm_rt_query("Red") q$stop("Harvard") q$date("2024-01-15")  headways_df <- q$headways() dwells_df   <- q$dwells() headways_df dwells_df

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: building query need know route line ID use. Rapid transit line IDs: "Red", "Orange", "Blue", "Green", "Mattapan". prefix form ("line-red") also accepted.
code: # All available routes, grouped by mode routes <- tm_routes() names(routes)  # "rapid_transit" "bus" "commuter_rail" "ferry"  # All bus route IDs (local lookup, no API call) tm_bus_routes()  # All commuter rail route IDs tm_cr_routes()  # All ferry route IDs tm_ferry_routes()

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: Every query object $stations() method returns data frame stops line route. Use find exact station name pass $stop(), $from_stop(), $to_stop(). can also call $directions() see direction names apply line:
code: # Red Line stations tm_rt_query("Red")$stations()  # Which commuter rail stops are on the Fairmount Line? tm_cr_query("CR-Fairmount")$stations()  # Route 66 bus stops tm_bus_query("66")$stations() tm_rt_query("Red")$directions() # 0 = "southbound", 1 = "northbound" (example)

what: Step 5: Fetch data for a single day
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: terminal methods need date ("YYYY-MM-DD" string Date object). can pass date directly terminal method set earlier $date().
code: # Headways at Harvard on the Red Line tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Harvard")$headways("2024-01-15")  # Dwell times at the same stop tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Harvard")$dwells("2024-01-15")  # Travel times — direction is required for travel time queries tm_rt_query("Red")$   from_stop("Alewife")$   to_stop("Kendall/MIT")$   direction("southbound")$   travel_times("2024-01-15")  # Speed restrictions on the Red Line for that day tm_rt_query("Red")$speed_restrictions("2024-01-15")  # Service alerts for a route on a specific day tm_alerts("2024-01-15", route = "Red")  # Current live alerts (no date needed) tm_alerts(route = "Red")

what: Step 6: Fetch data over a date range
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: multi-day analysis use $date_range(start, end) instead $date().
code: # Red Line ridership for January 2024 tm_rt_query("Red")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$ridership()  # Daily trip metrics for the Red Line tm_rt_query("Red")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$trip_metrics("daily")  # Delay summaries for the Red Line tm_rt_query("Red")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$line_delays()  # Revenue service hours (daily aggregation) tm_rt_query("Red")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$service_hours("daily")  # Scheduled service counts across all routes tm_scheduled_service("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily", route_id = "Red")

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: Aggregate endpoints return long-run averages date range, useful trend analysis.
code: # Average headways at Harvard over January 2024 tm_rt_query("Red")$   stop("Harvard")$   date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$   aggregate_headways()  # Average dwell times at Harvard tm_rt_query("Red")$   stop("Harvard")$   date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$   aggregate_dwells()  # Average travel times from Park Street to Davis tm_rt_query("Red")$   from_stop("Park Street")$   to_stop("Davis")$   date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$   aggregate_travel_times()

what: Step 8: Bus, Commuter Rail, and Ferry
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: builder pattern applies modes. Bus ferry support $headways(), $dwells(), $aggregate_headways(), $aggregate_dwells(). Commuter rail additionally supports $travel_times() $aggregate_travel_times(). Tip: Terminus stations commuter rail ferry may incomplete stop IDs one direction. $stop() warn pick terminus.
code: # Bus headways at Harvard on Route 66 tm_bus_query("66")$stop("Harvard")$headways("2024-01-15")  # Aggregate bus dwell times over a month tm_bus_query("1")$   stop("Hynes Station")$   date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$   aggregate_dwells()  # Commuter rail headways at Back Bay on the Fairmount Line tm_cr_query("CR-Fairmount")$stop("Newmarket")$headways("2026-01-15")  # Ferry headways at Hingham tm_ferry_query("Boat-F1")$stop("Hingham")$headways("2024-01-15")

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: Query methods return data frames, can pipe directly ggplot2:
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(transitmattr)  ridership_df <- tm_rt_query("Red")$   date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$   ridership()  ggplot(ridership_df, aes(x = as.Date(date), y = count)) +   geom_line(color = "#DA291C") +   # Red Line color   labs(     title = "Red Line Daily Ridership — January 2024",     x     = "Date",     y     = "Riders"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Recap: functions and methods at a glance
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: Standalone functions (query object needed): Query constructors → chain methods → call terminal method: Shared builder methods (query types): Terminal methods (fetch data, return data frame): Next, check Red Line Analysis vignette full worked example goes raw API data polished chart.

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: New packages? dplyr cleaning reshaping data; ggplot2 charts. Install install.packages(c("dplyr", "ggplot2")) needed.

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Build Red Line query, set date range, call $ridership(). result already data.frame — bind_rows() needed.

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: notice: Weekends typically show lower ridership. weekday unusually low, may service disruption — check tm_alerts() date.
code: ggplot(ridership_df, aes(x = as.Date(date), y = count)) +   geom_col(fill = "#DA291C") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title    = "Red Line Daily Ridership",     subtitle = "January 2024",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Riders"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: uses aggregate endpoint, gives long-run daily averages rather individual trips.

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Specify /station name. library handles stop ID resolution internally — need look "70076" "70064".

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Dashed line smooth trend. travel times creep time can signal slow zones accumulating — leads us section 3.
code: travel_df <- travel_df |>   mutate(     date       = as.Date(service_date),     travel_min = mean / 60   )  ggplot(travel_df, aes(x = date, y = travel_min)) +   geom_line(color = "#DA291C") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed",               color = "grey40") +   labs(     title    = "Average Travel Time: Park Street → Davis",     subtitle = "January 2024 · Red Line",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Minutes"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: 3. Slow zones on a specific day
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Speed restrictions (“slow zones”) places trains must go slower normal, usually track issues. Unlike endpoints, one returns list (data.frame) structure complex. Note: slow_raw$available FALSE, active slow zones day. Try different date check period known track work.
code: slow_raw <- tm_rt_query("Red")$speed_restrictions("2024-01-15") if (!isTRUE(slow_raw$available)) {   message("No slow zone data available for this date.") } else {   slow_df <- bind_rows(slow_raw$zones)   slow_df } ggplot(slow_df, aes(x = reorder(description, speedMph), y = speedMph, fill = speedMph)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#DA291C", high = "#f9c0c0") +   labs(     title    = "Red Line Slow Zones — January 15, 2024",     subtitle = "Lower mph = more restricted",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Speed limit (mph)"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: ’s pattern ’ll use transitmattr: ’re comfortable , try exploring methods: $line_delays() — delay minutes caused alerts $trip_metrics("daily") — -time performance per trip $aggregate_headways() — often trains come, time $service_hours("weekly") — scheduled vs. delivered service hours
code: tm_rt_query("Red")             # pick a line   $stop("Harvard")             # set a station   $date("2024-01-15")          # set a date   $headways()                  # fetch — returns a data.frame

what: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: R package TransitMatters Data Dashboard API, providing access MBTA performance metrics: headways, dwell times, travel times, ridership, speed restrictions, .

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: remotes:

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: # Check API health tm_healthcheck()  # List MBTA facilities tm_facilities()  # Service ridership dashboard summary tm_service_ridership_dashboard()

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: Functions accept either Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" string.
code: tm_headways("2024-01-15") tm_dwells(as.Date("2024-01-15")) tm_travel_times("2024-01-15")  # Dated or current alerts tm_alerts() tm_alerts("2024-01-15")

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: tm_aggregate_travel_times(   from_stop  = "place-pktrm",   to_stop    = "place-davis",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  tm_aggregate_headways(   stop       = "place-davis",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: # Ridership for the Red Line tm_ridership("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line_id = "Red")  # Delay summary by line tm_line_delays("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line = "Red")  # Trip metrics tm_trip_metrics("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily", line = "Red")  # Scheduled service (optionally filtered to one route) tm_scheduled_service("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily") tm_scheduled_service("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily", route_id = "Red")  # Speed restrictions / slow zones tm_speed_restrictions("Red", "2024-01-15")  # Service hours tm_service_hours("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily")

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: MIT

title: Get MBTA service alerts — tm_alerts
text: user_date NULL undated /api/alerts endpoint called, returning current live alerts. Pass date retrieve historical alerts, optionally filtered route.

title: Get MBTA service alerts — tm_alerts
code: tm_alerts(user_date = NULL, route = NULL, base_url = tm_base_url())

title: Get MBTA service alerts — tm_alerts
text: user_date Date object, "YYYY-MM-DD" string, NULL (default) current alerts. route One MBTA route IDs (e.g. "Red", "Orange"). used user_date provided. base_url Base URL TransitMatters API.

title: Get MBTA service alerts — tm_alerts
text: list alerts element.

title: Create a bus query — tm_bus_query
text: Constructs TmBusQuery object given MBTA bus route.

title: Create a bus query — tm_bus_query
text: route Bus route ID, e.g. "1", "66", "CT2", "SL1". Use tm_bus_routes() see valid route IDs. base_url API base URL. Defaults production host tm_dashboard_base_url option.

title: Create a commuter rail query — tm_cr_query
text: Constructs TmCRQuery object given MBTA commuter rail route. Calling $stop() terminus station issue warning stop IDs often missing one direction termini, making headway/dwell data potentially incomplete.

title: Create a commuter rail query — tm_cr_query

text: route CR route ID, e.g. "CR-Fairmount", "CR-Lowell". Use tm_cr_routes() see valid route IDs. base_url API base URL. Defaults production host tm_dashboard_base_url option.

what: Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
title: Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
text: Constructs TmRTQuery object given MBTA rapid transit line. Chain builder methods set context, call terminal method fetch data.

title: Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
text: line Line name: "Red", "Orange", "Blue", "Green", "Mattapan". Case-insensitive; "line-red" form also accepted. base_url API base URL. Defaults production host tm_dashboard_base_url option.

title: Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
text: TmRTQuery R6 object.

title: Create a rapid transit query — tm_rt_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Headways at Harvard on the Red Line tm_rt_query("Red")$stop("Harvard")$headways("2024-01-15")  # Travel times (direction required for stop ID resolution) tm_rt_query("Red")$from_stop("Alewife")$to_stop("Kendall/MIT")$direction("southbound")$travel_times("2024-01-15")  # Line-level aggregate (no stop needed) tm_rt_query("Red")$date_range("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31")$trip_metrics("daily") } # }

title: Get scheduled service data — tm_scheduled_service
text: Returns scheduled service counts aggregated date range.

title: Get scheduled service data — tm_scheduled_service
code: tm_scheduled_service(   start_date,   end_date,   agg,   route_id = NULL,   base_url = tm_base_url() )

title: Get scheduled service data — tm_scheduled_service
text: start_date Start date range. Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" string. end_date End date range. agg Aggregation level: "daily" "weekly". route_id Optional MBTA route ID (e.g. "Red", "Green-B"). NULL, returns data routes. base_url Base URL TransitMatters API.

title: Get scheduled service data — tm_scheduled_service
text: list scheduled service records.

what: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
title: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
text: thin 'httr2'-based wrapper around TransitMatters Data Dashboard API, providing access MBTA performance metrics including headways, dwell times, travel times, ridership, service data.

title: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
text: Maintainer: Andrew Kouré akoure@transitmatters.info Authors: Andrew Kouré akoure@transitmatters.info

title: transitmattr 0.2.0
text: mode-specific data functions (tm_headways(), tm_dwells(), tm_travel_times(), tm_ridership(), tm_trip_metrics(), tm_line_delays(), tm_service_hours(), tm_speed_restrictions(), tm_aggregate_headways(), tm_aggregate_dwells(), tm_aggregate_travel_times(), tm_aggregate_travel_times2()) removed. Use new query builder interface instead (see ). tm_time_predictions() now requires route_id argument. data-fetching functions previously returned list records. Query builder terminal methods now return data.frame directly, bind_rows() .call(rbind, ...) conversions longer needed.

title: transitmattr 0.2.0
text: Data now fetched mode-specific R6 query objects. object created constructor, configured chaining builder methods, activated terminal method: tm_rt_query(line) — rapid transit (Red, Orange, Blue, Green, Mattapan) tm_bus_query(route) — bus routes tm_cr_query(route) — commuter rail routes tm_ferry_query(route) — ferry routes Builder methods (return object invisibly chaining): $stop(), $from_stop(), $to_stop(), $direction(), $date(), $date_range() Discovery methods: $stations() $directions() return data frames available stops/directions without hitting data API. Terminal methods (return data.frame): $headways(), $dwells(), $travel_times(), $aggregate_headways(), $aggregate_dwells(), $aggregate_travel_times(), $ridership(), $trip_metrics(), $line_delays(), $service_hours(), $scheduled_service(), $speed_restrictions() Commuter rail ferry terminus stations now emit warning selected via $stop(), $from_stop(), $to_stop(), stop IDs may missing one direction.

title: transitmattr 0.2.0
text: tm_bus_routes() — returns sorted character vector supported bus route IDs (local lookup, API call). tm_cr_routes() — commuter rail routes. tm_ferry_routes() — ferry routes.

title: transitmattr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.
